zh.lifeSummary: 當Arnold I Robertsz van Heusden van Rode於1060年出生在比利時福拉阿姆-布拉班特辛吉根修-洛德時，他的父親Robert IV van Heusden15歲，母親Johanna Van Altena4歲。他於大約1085年與Heylwiva de Walbeck在荷蘭努-布拉班特辛特 安東尼努德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1135年享年75歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Arnold I Robertsz van Heusden van Rode于1060年出生在比利时福拉阿姆-布拉班特辛吉根修-洛德时，他的父亲Robert IV van Heusden15岁，母亲Johanna Van Altena4岁。他于大约1085年与Heylwiva de Walbeck在荷兰努-布拉班特辛特 安東尼努德结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1135年享年75岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Heusden
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Ousden (Suffolk) with prosthetic initial H-. The placename derives from Old English ūf ‘owl’ (genitive ūfes) + denu ‘valley’.

nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Rode
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Erwin, Ewald, Hans, Heinz, Oskar, Viktor, Claus, Frieda, Fritz, Gerhard, Horst.
German and French: from a short form of any of various ancient Germanic personal names with the first element hrōd ‘renown’. Compare Robert , Rudiger .

North German, Danish, and English: topographic name for someone who lived on land cleared for cultivation or in a clearing in woodland, from Middle Low German rode, Danish rothe, Old English rod. There are a number of places in England named with this word, for example Rode in Cheshire, and the surname may derive from any of these. Compare Rhode .

variants.name: Ruth
meanings: German: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names formed with hrōd ‘renown’ (see Rode ).
(Rüth): habitational name from a place so named near Euskirchen in Rhineland.

English: from Middle English reuth(e) ‘pity’, a nickname perhaps for a charitable person or for a pitiable one. The personal name Ruth was little used in England in the Middle Ages among non-Jews, and is unlikely to have had any influence on the surname.

variants.name: Rody
meanings: German: variant of   Rode   1.
Scottish: variant of Rodie, from a pet form of Roderick .

variants.name: Robert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Marcel, Gilles, Normand, Armand, Lucien, Pierre, Emile, Michel, Edouard, Gaston, Yves.

French, Walloon, English, German, Dutch, Flemish, and Breton (of French origin); Hungarian (Róbert): from the ancient Germanic personal name Ro(d)bert (Middle English Robert, Old French Ro(d)bert), composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This is found occasionally in Anglo-Saxon England before the Conquest, but in the main it was introduced into England by the Normans and quickly became popular among all classes of society. Pet forms and diminutives are found from an early date, Robin being particularly common. Compare Robair , Robar , Robare , and Rober .
In some cases also an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Rodeman
meanings: Americanized form of German Rodemann:
topographic name for someone who lived on woodland cleared for cultivation, a variant of Rode 2 with the addition of Middle Low German man ‘man’.
from an ancient Germanic personal name, Hrodman, formed with hrōd ‘renown, fame’ + man ‘man’.

variants.name: Winterroth
meanings: German: topographic name for someone who lived on the north side of a clearing (see   Rode  ).

variants.name: Roth
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for a person with red hair, from Middle High German rōt, German rot ‘red’. As a Jewish name it is at least in part artificial: its frequency as a Jewish surname is disproportionate to the number of Jews who, one may reasonably assume, were red-headed during the period of surname adoption. This form of the German surname (especially in this sense and in the sense 2 below) is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), where it is most common, and in some other European countries, e.g. Czechia and Croatia.

German and English (Middlesex): topographic name for someone who lived on land that had been cleared, from Old High German rod, Middle English roth(e) (Old English roth) ‘clearing’. In England, the name may also be a habitational name from any of the places like Rothend in Ashdon (Essex), Roe End in Markyate (Hertfordshire), Roe Green in Hatfield (Hertfordshire), or Roe Green in Sandon (Hertfordshire).
German: from a short form of any of various ancient Germanic personal names with the first element hrōd ‘renown’. Compare Rode 1, Ross 4.

variants.name: Rohde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Kurt, Otto, Bernd, Hans, Heino, Heinz, Ewald, Gerhard, Helmut, Hermann, Klaus.
German and Danish: variant of   Rode   2 and a habitational name from any of various places called Rohde or Rohden in Lower Saxony, Saxony, Westphalia, and Hesse. Compare   Rhode   and   Rhody  .

variants.name: Roady
meanings: Perhaps an Americanized form of German   Rode  .

variants.name: Rodich
meanings: Americanized form of Serbian and Croatian Rodić: patronymic from a short form of a Slavic compound personal name based on the element rod ‘family, relatives’, e.g. Rodoslav.
Americanized form of Slovenian Rodič: surname probably related to (or derived from) Rode . Alternatively, it may be of the same origin as the Serbian and Croatian name above.
German: perhaps a variant of Rohdich, from a personal name based on ancient Germanic hrōd ‘renown, victory’.

nameMeanings.name: Arnold
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  Jewish: Yiddish contracted form of   Aaron .
2  English: short form of   Arnold .

nameMeanings.name: Robertsz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the personal name Robert , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is also occasionally borne by Jews, presumably as an Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

variants.name: Robert
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

variants.name: Robers
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

variants.name: Roberds
meanings: English: variant of   Roberts  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Robards
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Roberts  .

variants.name: Robarts
meanings: English (Bedfordshire and Hertfortshire): variant of   Roberts  .

en.lifeSummary: When Arnold I Robertsz van Heusden van Rode was born in 1060, in Sint-Genesius-Rode, Flemish Brabant, Belgium, his father, Robert IV van Heusden, was 15 and his mother, Johanna Van Altena, was 4. He married Heylwiva de Walbeck about 1085, in Noord, Sint Anthonis, North Brabant, Netherlands. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1135, at the age of 75.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Arnold I Robertsz van Heusden van Rode est né en 1060, à Sint-Genesius-Rode, Brabant Flamand, Belgique, son père, Robert IV van Heusden, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Johanna Van Altena, avait 4 ans. Il a épousé Heylwiva de Walbeck environ 1085, à Noord, Sint Anthonis, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1135, à l’âge de 75 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Arnold I Robertsz van Heusden van Rode 1060 in Sint-Genesius-Rode, Flämisch Brabant, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert IV van Heusden 15 und seine Mutter Johanna Van Altena 4. Er hat um 1085 in Noord, Sint Anthonis, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande Heylwiva de Walbeck geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1135 im Alter von 75 Jahren.

it.St Oedenrode, North Brabant, Netherlands: Noord, Sint Anthonis, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi
it.lifeSummary: Quando Arnold I Robertsz van Heusden van Rode è nato il nel 1060 nel Sint-Genesius-Rode, Vlaams-Brabant, Belgio, il suo padre, Robert IV van Heusden, aveva 15 anni e il suo madre, Johanna Van Altena, aveva 4 anni. Lui sposato Heylwiva de Walbeck circa 1085, nel Noord, Sint Anthonis, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1135, all'età di 75 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Arnold I Robertsz van Heusden van Rodeが1060年にベルギーvラアムス-ブラバントシント-ジェンエシウス-ロドで生まれた時，父Robert IV van Heusdenは15歳，母Johanna Van Altenaは4歳であった。約1085年にオランダノオルド-ブラバントシント アンソニスノオルドでHeylwiva de Walbeckと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1135年に75歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Arnold I Robertsz van Heusden van Rode(이)가 1060년 벨기에 블라아스-브라반트 신트-겐시우스-로드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert IV van Heusden(은)는 15세였고, 그의 어머니 Johanna Van Altena(은)는 4세였다. 그는 대략 1085년 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 신트 앤토니스 노르드에서 Heylwiva de Walbeck(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1135년 75세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Arnold I Robertsz van Heusden van Rode nasceu em 1060, em Sint-Genesius-Rode, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, seu pai, Robert IV van Heusden, tinha 15 anos e sua mãe, Johanna Van Altena, tinha 4 anos. Ele casou-se com Heylwiva de Walbeck aproximadamente 1085, em Noord, Sint Anthonis, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1135, com 75 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Arnold I Robertsz van Heusden van Rode родился в 1060 г. в местности синт-джинесюс-род, влаамс-брабант, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Robert IV van Heusden было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Johanna Van Altena, 4. Он женился на женщине по имени Heylwiva de Walbeck около 1085 г. в местности нурд, синт антонис, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1135 г. в возрасте 75 лет.

Se casó con Heylwiva de Walbeck aproximadamente 1085, en Noord, Sint Anthonis, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1135, a la edad de 75 años.
